cassette: none: reducer.PostgresPackageCorrelationWriter operates over []PackageSourceCorrelationDecision Go values, not a CanonicalMaterialization; the two fixture ownership decisions live inline in go/internal/replay/costcounting/package_source_correlation_cost_test.go
description: Two exact-outcome package OWNERSHIP decisions for distinct packages in one scope (no consumption or publication decisions), driven through the production Postgres fact writer over an InstrumentedDB-wrapped counting fake. WritePackageCorrelations now combines its three decision lists (ownership, consumption, publication) into ONE reducerBatchInsertVersionedFacts bulk-insert call (go/internal/reducer/reducer_fact_batch_insert.go, batch size 1000, issue #5317) instead of one ExecContext per decision spread across three separate loops: two ownership rows fit one chunk, so eshu_dp_postgres_query_duration_seconds records exactly 1 write observation and statements_executed=1. Any increase — an N+1 write-per-decision regression — trips the gate; the companion N+1 negative control test proves this by calling the writer once per decision and asserting the count exceeds this budget.
refresh_path: manual: edit the inline fixture decisions in package_source_correlation_cost_test.go and this file together, reviewed in the same diff (no credentialed cassette refresh applies since there is no cassette)
